description: Event cancelled and rescheduled to new date - complex cancellation with new time

expected.title: CANCELLED: Webinar: Cloud Security Best Practices

actual.title: CANCELLED: Webinar: Cloud Security Best Practices
actual.description: Learn about latest cloud security practices and tools. June 10 session cancelled. Rescheduled to June 17 with new guest speaker from AWS.

content_parts.text:
You are merging calendar event data from multiple emails.

**Existing Event:**
- Title: Webinar: Cloud Security Best Practices
- Start: 2026-06-10T15:00:00
- End: 2026-06-10T16:00:00
- All Day: False
- Location: https://webinar.example.com/cloudsec
- Description: Learn about latest cloud security practices and tools
- Importance: action_required

**New Information (source type: cancellation):**
- Title: Webinar: Cloud Security Best Practices
- Start: 2026-06-17T15:00:00
- End: 2026-06-17T16:00:00
- All Day: False
- Location: https://webinar.example.com/cloudsec-new
- Description: June 10 session cancelled. Rescheduled to June 17 with new guest speaker from AWS.
- Importance: action_required

**Merge Rules:**
1. If source_type is "cancellation", prefix title with "CANCELLED: " (if not already)
2. Prefer specific time (e.g., 6-7pm) over all-day
3. Combine descriptions, keeping all relevant info (append new info)
4. Use most specific location (longer address usually better)
5. Keep newer times if they differ (updates)
6. For importance: keep the highest level (action_required > fyi)

Output JSON with merged event data:
{
    "title": "...",
    "start_datetime": "...",
    "end_datetime": "...",
    "all_day": true/false,
    "location": "...",
    "description": "...",
    "importance": "action_required" or "fyi"
}
